title: Supporting Veteran Families in Need Act
short_title: Supporting Veteran Families in Need Act
stage_label: Referred to the Subcommittee on Economic Opportunity.
plain_summary: This bill makes a program for veteran families permanent. The program helps very low-income veterans and their families who live in permanent homes or are trying to get permanent homes. It gives them money for services they need to help them stay housed. The program will keep getting funding starting in 2027 and every year after that. This means veteran families will always have this help available to them.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Economic Opportunity.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Supporting Veteran Families in Need Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Permanent Authority to Provide Financial Assistance for Supportive Services for Very Low-Income Veteran Families in Permanent Housing
sections.text: This section changes an existing law about helping veteran families. The Department of Veterans Affairs helps very low-income veteran families who have permanent homes by paying for supportive services. These services help families stay in their homes and meet their needs. Right now, this program has an ending date. This change makes the program permanent by allowing Congress to provide money for the program starting in fiscal year two thousand twenty seven and every year after that. The section also reorganizes how the funding rules are numbered in the existing law.
